dashboard.greetingSub: You're on track. GS3 Economy session up next today.

currentAffairs.heroSub: 7 news items • mapped to UPSC syllabus • GS1 through GS4 + Essay
currentAffairs.news1Title: RBI keeps Repo Rate unchanged at 6.5% — signals 'wait and watch'
currentAffairs.news1Desc: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive time, signaling a continued focus on bringing inflation durably down to the 4% target before considering rate cuts.
currentAffairs.news1Links: GS3 — Monetary Policy Tools (Repo, CRR, SLR) · MPC Composition · GS2 — RBI as a Statutory Body
currentAffairs.news2Title: India-Maldives Relations: Foreign Minister visits New Delhi, signs 3 MoUs
currentAffairs.news2Desc: The visit by Maldives Foreign Minister Moosa Zameer to New Delhi marks a potential thaw in diplomatic ties after months of strain following President Muizzu's pro-China pivot.
currentAffairs.news2Links: GS2 — India's 'Neighbourhood First' Policy · Indian Ocean Geopolitics · GS3 — Blue Economy
currentAffairs.news3Title: Forest Rights Act — Supreme Court upholds Gram Sabha authority over forest land diversion
currentAffairs.news3Desc: The Supreme Court has clarified that Gram Sabha consent is mandatory under the Forest Rights Act, 2006, prior to the diversion of forest land for development projects. The ruling will have sweeping implications for several pending infrastructure projects in tribal belts.
currentAffairs.news3Links: GS1 — Tribal Society · GS2 — 5th & 6th Schedule · PESA Act · GS3 — Forest Rights vs Development · GS4 — Environmental Ethics

studyContent.title: Study Content
studyContent.subtitle: Syllabus-aligned notes • PYQ Linked • Multiple formats
studyContent.q1Body1: Fiscal policy refers to the use of government revenue collection (taxation) and expenditure to influence a country's economy. In India, the Union Budget, presented annually on February 1st, is the primary instrument of fiscal policy.
studyContent.q1Body2: Unlike monetary policy (which is controlled by the RBI), fiscal policy is controlled by the elected government — making it inherently political as well as economic.
studyContent.pyqQuestion1: "Discuss the role of fiscal policy in India's post-pandemic economic recovery. What structural reforms should be prioritized to achieve sustainable growth?"
studyContent.bullet1: Capital Expenditure (productive, asset-creating) vs Revenue Expenditure (subsidies, salaries). Union Budget 2024-25 allocated ₹11.11 lakh crore for infrastructure.
studyContent.bullet2: Deficit Financing: When expenditure exceeds revenue. Fiscal Deficit target: 5.1% of GDP (2024-25).
studyContent.q3Title: 3. The FRBM Act and Fiscal Consolidation
studyContent.pyqQuestion2: "The FRBM Act has failed to achieve its stated objectives. Do you agree? Analyse."
studyContent.keyData: Key Data Points for Answer Writing

prelimsLab.title: Prelims Lab
prelimsLab.subtitle: 1,240 solved · 71% accuracy · Economy and S&T are weak areas
prelimsLab.qQuestion: With reference to the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of India, consider the following statements:
prelimsLab.qS1: 1. It has six members - three from RBI and three external experts nominated by the Central Government.
prelimsLab.qS2: 2. Decisions are taken by majority vote, with the Governor having a casting vote in case of a tie.
prelimsLab.qS3: 3. It meets at least four times a year as mandated by the RBI Act.
prelimsLab.qPrompt: Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsLab.insightText: Economy accuracy is 58% — below cutoff safety threshold. Next session will serve 60% Economy questions to close the gap.

community.title: Community
community.subtitle: 4,280 active aspirants · Doubt-clear, peer review, study groups
community.post1Title: Difference between Repo Rate and Reverse Repo Rate in policy transmission?
community.post1Desc: I understand the definitions, but struggle to explain how a 25bp repo rate cut actually transmits to home loan EMIs in practice. Can someone explain the step-by-step mechanism with real numbers? The RBI-bank-customer chain is confusing me.
community.post2Title: My GS2 Federalism answer got 21/25 in peer review — sharing what worked
community.post2Desc: Peer review was invaluable. The key feedback: I was listing facts but not "critically examining." The structure that worked: Context para → Issue from each dimension (legislative/executive/financial) → SC judgements → Way forward with specific recommendations. Posting my answer in comments.

wellbeing.subtitle: Your Wellbeing Centre
wellbeing.desc: UPSC is a long game. Sustainable study beats burnout every time.
wellbeing.moodSub: Takes 10 seconds. We'll adjust your plan if needed.
wellbeing.moodAlert: Great! You're feeling good. Full plan active for today. Keep this energy going.
wellbeing.insightText: You're studying efficiently. Average session length is 87 minutes — healthy for retention. Wednesday was your most productive day (4.2 hrs). Consider similar schedule Fridays.
wellbeing.slowDesc: Having a difficult week? Activate this — we reduce your daily targets by 40% for 5 days, no guilt. UPSC is a marathon.
wellbeing.storiesTitle: Real Stories — Because You Need to Hear These
wellbeing.story1Text: "Failed 4 times. My family stopped asking about it. I started treating each failure as data, not shame. AIR 89 in my 5th attempt."
wellbeing.story2Text: "I was working a full-time job. 3 hours a day, 6 days a week. The difference was consistency over intensity. No 14-hour days."
wellbeing.story3Text: "Answer writing was terrifying at first. I wrote 200 answers before Mains. The fear went away. The scores didn't matter — the practice did."

welcome.specimenTitle: Every wrong answer is a guide.
welcome.specimenSub: We don't just tell you what's correct. We match your mistake against 84,000 other aspirants who chose the same option, then lead you to the exact NCERT page, exact text, exact PYQ that clears the misconception.
welcome.specimenPoint2: Option-elimination logic shown for every question
welcome.qText: With reference to the Cabinet Mission of 1946, which of the following statements is/are correct?
welcome.qS1: 1. It recommended a federal government with a three-tier classification of provinces.
welcome.qS2: 2. It rejected the idea of a separate Constituent Assembly for the Muslim League.
welcome.qS3: 3. It proposed an interim government drawn from the existing Central Legislative Assembly.
welcome.qWhyB: Statement 1 is correct (the Cabinet Mission's three-tier grouping was federal). Statement 2 is incorrect (it accepted a single Constituent Assembly). Statement 3 is correct (the interim government was drawn from members of the existing central assembly).

auth.bioPlaceholder: Tell us about your UPSC journey...

askAI.dek: Trained on the UPSC syllabus, fact-checked against PYQs and standard sources. Cites everything. Won't bluff. Tries to teach, not just answer.
askAI.inputPlaceholder: Ask anything — concept, PYQ, current affairs link…
askAI.factText: Every claim is cross-checked against the syllabus corpus. If I can't cite, I won't say. If a PYQ answer key conflicts with NCERT, I'll flag it openly.
